_meta.description: Personalized recommendations for each Prakriti type. Based on classical Ayurvedic texts: Charaka Samhita, Ashtanga Hridayam, and Sushruta Samhita.

Vata.name: Vata
Vata.tagline: The force of movement and creativity
Vata.heroDescription: You are the wind — ever-moving, endlessly creative, and brilliantly alive. Your mind races ahead of the crowd, your imagination knows no limits, and your spirit craves adventure. When in balance, you are the spark that lights up any room. Your gift to the world is creativity, intuition, and enthusiasm.

principles: Eat warm, cooked, oily, and nourishing foods
Favor regular meal times — consistency calms Vata
Eat slowly and mindfully in a calm environment
Avoid raw, cold, and dry foods especially in winter
Small, frequent meals work better than large ones

favorFoods.name: Warm milk with ghee
favorFoods.reason: Deeply nourishing and calming for Vata

favorFoods.name: Sweet potatoes
favorFoods.reason: Grounding, sweet, and easy to digest

favorFoods.name: Sesame oil
favorFoods.reason: Warming and lubricating for dry Vata tissues

favorFoods.name: Basmati rice
favorFoods.reason: Light yet nourishing, easy on digestion

favorFoods.name: Cooked vegetables
favorFoods.reason: Easy to digest when cooked with warming spices

favorFoods.name: Dates and figs
favorFoods.reason: Sweet, moist, and deeply nourishing

favorFoods.name: Mung dal
favorFoods.reason: Easy to digest, warming, grounding protein

favorFoods.name: Warm herbal teas
favorFoods.reason: Ginger, cardamom, cinnamon teas calm and warm

favorFoods.name: Avocado
favorFoods.reason: Rich, oily, and grounding for Vata's dryness

favorFoods.name: Warm soups and stews
favorFoods.reason: The ideal Vata meal — warm, moist, nourishing

minimizeFoods.name: Cold drinks and ice
minimizeFoods.reason: Aggravates Vata's cold and dry quality

minimizeFoods.name: Raw salads
minimizeFoods.reason: Hard to digest, increases dryness and gas

minimizeFoods.name: Dried fruits
minimizeFoods.reason: Too dry — soak them first if you eat them

minimizeFoods.name: Crackers and dry snacks
minimizeFoods.reason: Increase dryness and are hard to digest

minimizeFoods.name: Carbonated drinks
minimizeFoods.reason: Gas and bloating are Vata's biggest weakness

routine.principle: Regularity is medicine for Vata. Fixed times for waking, eating, and sleeping are your greatest tools for balance.

daily.detail: Vata time is 2–6 AM. Rising just before sunrise harnesses creative energy.
10–15 min full body massage with warm sesame oil. Deeply grounding for Vata.
Wash off oil. Keep it warm — never cold showers for Vata.
30 min of grounding yoga or a slow walk in nature. Avoid intense cardio in the morning.
Calms the restless Vata mind. Focus on breath or body scan.
Oatmeal, warm porridge, or eggs. Never skip breakfast — Vata needs fuel to start.
Eat your biggest meal at noon when digestion is strongest. Warm, cooked food.
Light, warm, and easy to digest. Finish by 7 PM ideally.
Vata's biggest challenge is sleep. Dim lights, calming music, or reading.
A cup of warm milk with nutmeg and cardamom deeply supports Vata sleep.

weeklyPractices: Shirodhara or head oil massage once a week
Warm bath with essential oils (lavender, sandalwood)
Digital detox for half a day
Spend time in nature — forests and gardens

yoga.principle: Slow, grounding, and warming poses. Hold poses longer. Focus on stability and connection to the earth.
yoga.style: Slow Flow, Yin Yoga, or Hatha
yoga.intensity: Low to moderate — avoid vigorous or fast-paced styles

poses.name: Mountain Pose
poses.benefit: Grounds Vata's airy nature, builds stability

poses.name: Warrior I
poses.benefit: Builds strength and focus, counters anxiety

poses.name: Child's Pose
poses.benefit: Deeply calming and grounding — Vata's best friend

poses.name: Seated Forward Bend
poses.benefit: Calms the nervous system, releases tension

poses.name: Legs Up the Wall
poses.benefit: Deeply restorative, calms anxiety and insomnia

yoga.breathwork: Nadi Shodhana (alternate nostril breathing) — best pranayama for Vata. Calms the nervous system deeply.
yoga.avoid: Avoid Bikram/hot yoga, fast Vinyasa, and intense power yoga — too depleting for Vata

autumn.focus: This is peak Vata season — dry, windy, variable. Your highest-risk period.
tips: Daily warm oil massage is non-negotiable in autumn
Eat soups, stews, and root vegetables heavily
Keep warm — never let yourself get cold
Maintain very regular sleep and meal times
Reduce travel and major life changes if possible

winter.focus: Stay warm and nourished. Winter calls for rich, warming foods.
tips: Favor warming spices: ginger, cinnamon, black pepper
Sesame and almond oil for daily massage
More sleep is natural and healthy in winter
Hot herbal teas throughout the day

spring.focus: Enjoy this balancing season. Gradually introduce lighter foods.
tips: A gentle spring cleanse is beneficial
Begin introducing more fresh foods slowly
Increase outdoor activity as weather warms
This is a great time to start new habits

summer.focus: Vata is most balanced in summer. Enjoy it but avoid overheating.
tips: Coconut oil is ideal for summer massage
Stay hydrated with room-temperature water
Enjoy fresh, sweet fruits freely
Morning exercise before 10 AM

Pitta.name: Pitta
Pitta.tagline: The force of transformation and intelligence
Pitta.heroDescription: You are fire — sharp, brilliant, and unstoppable when focused. Your mind cuts through complexity, your drive gets things done, and your passion is contagious. When in balance, you are the leader others follow, the problem-solver people call on, and the one who turns ideas into reality. Your gift is intelligence, courage, and transformation.

principles: Favor cooling, sweet, and bitter foods to balance heat
Never skip meals — hungry Pitta becomes angry Pitta
Eat in a calm environment without rushing
Avoid very spicy, oily, sour, or fermented foods
Room temperature or cool (not ice cold) drinks are ideal

favorFoods.name: Coconut water
favorFoods.reason: Cooling and hydrating — Pitta's perfect drink

favorFoods.name: Sweet fruits (mango, melon)
favorFoods.reason: Naturally cooling and satisfying for Pitta

favorFoods.name: Cucumber
favorFoods.reason: Hydrating and cooling, reduces internal heat

favorFoods.name: Coriander and fennel
favorFoods.reason: Cooling spices that balance Pitta's fire

favorFoods.name: Ghee
favorFoods.reason: Cooling (unlike other fats), deeply nourishing

favorFoods.name: Milk and dairy (in moderation)
favorFoods.reason: Sweet and cooling when used appropriately

favorFoods.name: Leafy greens
favorFoods.reason: Bitter taste directly reduces Pitta

favorFoods.name: Basmati rice
favorFoods.reason: Light, sweet, and cooling

favorFoods.name: Pomegranate
favorFoods.reason: Astringent and cooling, excellent for Pitta

favorFoods.name: Mint tea
favorFoods.reason: Cooling and calming for Pitta's heat

minimizeFoods.name: Chilli and hot spices
minimizeFoods.reason: Directly increases Pitta heat and inflammation

minimizeFoods.name: Alcohol
minimizeFoods.reason: Heating and aggravating — especially in excess

minimizeFoods.name: Fried and oily food
minimizeFoods.reason: Increases heat and aggravates the liver

minimizeFoods.name: Red meat
minimizeFoods.reason: Heavy and heating — occasional is fine

minimizeFoods.name: Caffeine in excess
minimizeFoods.reason: Heating, acidic, and stimulating — hard on Pitta

routine.principle: Cooling down and letting go are the two most important practices for Pitta. Build spaciousness into your day.

daily.activity: Wake up — before the sun gets hot
daily.detail: Pitta time is 10 AM–2 PM. Morning is your window for cool, focused work.
Cooling oils calm Pitta's heat. Avoid heavy sesame oil.
Avoid overheating — exercise in the cool of the morning. Swimming is ideal.
Cooling meditations (moon visualization, loving-kindness) balance Pitta's fire.
Sweet fruits, cool milk, or light grains. Not too heavy.
Pitta digestion is strongest at noon. Eat well but not to excess.
Avoid intense work from 2–4 PM when Pitta heat peaks.
Sweet, easy to digest. Done by 7:30 PM.

daily.activity: Wind down — creative or reflective time
daily.detail: Pitta often pushes too late. Protect your evenings from work.
The Pitta night window (10 PM–2 AM) can trap you in alertness if you're still awake.

weeklyPractices: One full nature day — forest, beach, or mountains
Cold water swimming or moonlit walks
Deliberately leave one task unfinished (practice letting go)

yoga.principle: Cooling, surrendering, and non-competitive. Focus on releasing control and finding joy in the practice.
yoga.style: Gentle Hatha, Yin Yoga, Moon Salutations
yoga.intensity: Moderate — avoid competitive or hot yoga

poses.name: Moon Salutation
poses.benefit: Cooling counterpart to the Sun Salutation

poses.name: Fish Pose
poses.benefit: Opens chest, releases tension from the solar plexus

poses.name: Seated Spinal Twist
poses.benefit: Detoxifies liver and spleen, key Pitta organs

poses.name: Corpse Pose (extended)
poses.benefit: Full surrender — hardest pose for Pitta, most needed

poses.name: Forward Fold
poses.benefit: Cooling, calms the nervous system and eyes

yoga.breathwork: Sheetali (cooling breath) — breathing through a curled tongue or Chandra Bhedana (left nostril breathing). Both directly cool Pitta.
yoga.avoid: Avoid Bikram/hot yoga, intense power yoga, breath of fire (Kapalabhati) — all increase Pitta heat

summer.focus: Peak Pitta season — your highest-risk time. Prioritize cooling above all.
tips: Eat cooling foods: coconut, cucumber, sweet fruits
Avoid the midday sun between 11 AM–3 PM
Swimming and moonlit walks are your best exercise
Rose water, sandalwood, and coconut oil for skin
Reduce alcohol, coffee, and spicy food completely

spring.focus: A good season for Pitta — use it for gentle cleansing.
tips: Spring Panchakarma cleanse is ideal for Pitta
Introduce bitter greens and cleansing foods
Great time for new projects — harness Pitta's drive
Keep exercising but choose cool morning hours

autumn.focus: Pitta naturally reduces — enjoy the natural balance.
tips: Enjoy warming, nourishing foods as weather cools
This is Pitta's most productive and balanced season
Great time for ambitious projects and goals
Harvest and celebration foods are well-tolerated

winter.focus: Pitta's rest season — allow the fire to bank and restore.
tips: Enjoy rich, warming foods — you can handle more
Rest more and reduce pressure on yourself
Warm sesame oil massage is fine in winter
Reflect, plan, and restore for the year ahead

Kapha.name: Kapha
Kapha.tagline: The force of stability, love, and endurance
Kapha.heroDescription: You are the earth — stable, nurturing, and endlessly patient. When others panic, you are calm. When others give up, you endure. Your love is deep, your loyalty is unshakeable, and your presence is a gift to everyone around you. You are the foundation that holds communities together. Your gift is love, steadiness, and the strength to endure.
Kapha.famousVataTypes: Teachers, nurses, counselors, athletes (endurance), community leaders

principles: Light, dry, warm, and stimulating foods are your ideal
Avoid heavy, oily, cold, and sweet foods in excess
Eat your smallest meal at dinner — Kapha digestion slows at night
Favor spicy, bitter, astringent tastes that stimulate metabolism
Intermittent fasting works well for Kapha types

favorFoods.name: Ginger and black pepper
favorFoods.reason: Stimulating spices that fire up Kapha's slow digestion

favorFoods.name: Honey (raw, not heated)
favorFoods.reason: The only sweet that reduces Kapha — scraping and light

favorFoods.name: Light grains (millet, barley)
favorFoods.reason: Lighter and drier than rice or wheat — ideal for Kapha

favorFoods.name: Legumes
favorFoods.reason: High protein, astringent, and relatively dry

favorFoods.name: Leafy greens
favorFoods.reason: Bitter and light — exactly what Kapha needs

favorFoods.name: Warm water with lemon
favorFoods.reason: Morning ritual that stimulates sluggish Kapha digestion

favorFoods.name: Pomegranate and berries
favorFoods.reason: Astringent fruits that are well-tolerated by Kapha

favorFoods.name: Turmeric
favorFoods.reason: Anti-inflammatory, stimulating, a Kapha superstar spice

favorFoods.name: Cruciferous vegetables
favorFoods.reason: Light, bitter, and metabolism-boosting

favorFoods.name: Herbal teas (ginger, tulsi)
favorFoods.reason: Warm, stimulating, and decongesting

minimizeFoods.name: Dairy (especially cold)
minimizeFoods.reason: Heavy and mucus-forming — major Kapha aggravator

minimizeFoods.name: Wheat and bread
minimizeFoods.reason: Heavy and building — switch to lighter grains

minimizeFoods.name: Sugar and sweets
minimizeFoods.reason: Directly increases Kapha heaviness and weight

minimizeFoods.name: Cold, iced drinks
minimizeFoods.reason: Slows already slow Kapha metabolism further

minimizeFoods.name: Deep fried food
minimizeFoods.reason: Heavy, oily — the worst combination for Kapha

minimizeFoods.name: Excessive salt
minimizeFoods.reason: Water retention and heaviness increase with salt

routine.principle: Movement, stimulation, and variety are medicine for Kapha. Fight the urge to be comfortable — push yourself gently every single day.

daily.activity: Wake up early — before Kapha time begins
daily.detail: Kapha time is 6–10 AM. Waking before this window avoids the heavy, slow energy.
Dry silk or raw cotton glove massage stimulates lymph and circulation — ideal for Kapha.
Kapha needs to sweat. Running, cycling, or vigorous yoga for 45–60 min.
Ending with a brief cool rinse stimulates Kapha's circulation.
Kapha benefits from activating meditation — visualizations, affirmations, or chanting.

daily.activity: Light breakfast — or skip if not hungry
daily.detail: Fruit, a light smoothie, or ginger tea. Kapha can easily skip breakfast.
Spiced, warm, but not heavy. Avoid afternoon drowsiness by not overeating.
Avoid the Kapha afternoon slump (2–4 PM) with gentle movement.
Light soup or salad. No heavy dinners for Kapha — digestion slows at night.
7–8 hours is ideal. Oversleeping increases Kapha heaviness.

weeklyPractices: Try one completely new activity or route each week
Social engagement — Kapha thrives in community
Sauna or steam room to clear congestion
Declutter one area of your home or workspace

yoga.principle: Vigorous, energizing, and warming. Push past the resistance to start — once moving, Kapha thrives.
yoga.style: Power Yoga, Vinyasa Flow, or Sun Salutations
yoga.intensity: High — Kapha needs to sweat and work hard

poses.name: Sun Salutation (fast-paced)
poses.benefit: Full-body energizer — the single best practice for Kapha

poses.name: Camel Pose
poses.benefit: Opens chest, counters Kapha's tendency to collapse inward

poses.name: Boat Pose
poses.benefit: Fires up digestion and core strength

poses.name: Plank Pose
poses.benefit: Builds heat and core strength rapidly

yoga.breathwork: Kapalabhati (breath of fire) — rapid diaphragmatic breathing. Directly stimulates and decongests Kapha. Do 3 rounds of 30–50 breaths each morning.
yoga.avoid: Avoid Yin Yoga, long Savasana (extended), and any very slow or restorative practice — increases Kapha inertia

spring.focus: Peak Kapha season — congestion, allergies, and heaviness peak. Most important season for Kapha.
tips: Do a spring cleanse — this is your most beneficial time
Dramatically reduce dairy, wheat, and sweets
Increase exercise intensity in spring
Use neti pot daily for sinus congestion
Honey with ginger every morning is powerful medicine

winter.focus: Cold and damp increases Kapha. Stay active and warm inside.
tips: Never miss your morning exercise in winter
Warming spices: ginger, pepper, cinnamon in everything
Avoid cold food entirely in winter
Stay socially active — isolation worsens Kapha depression

summer.focus: Summer naturally reduces Kapha — your most balanced season.
tips: Enjoy lighter foods and outdoor activity freely
This is your most energetic and positive season
Summer is ideal for travel and new experiences
Take advantage of high energy for ambitious projects

autumn.focus: Good season for Kapha — crisp and dry weather is balancing.
tips: Enjoy the season but watch for Kapha foods (Halloween sweets!)
Root vegetables and warming soups are ideal
Maintain exercise routine as days get shorter
Use this time to establish healthy winter habits

Vata-Pitta.name: Vata-Pitta
Vata-Pitta.heroDescription: You are the rarest of combinations — creative fire and sharp brilliance combined. You think fast, act fast, and feel everything intensely. Your mind is your superpower and your greatest challenge. When balanced, you are an unstoppable creative force. Your twin enemies are anxiety (from Vata) and frustration (from Pitta) — managing both is your lifelong practice.
Vata-Pitta.primaryBalance: Ground the Vata, cool the Pitta. Regularity (Vata) + cooling (Pitta) is your daily medicine.
keyPractices: Regular meal times (Vata) with cooling foods (Pitta)
Consistent sleep schedule — both doshas suffer from poor sleep
Moderate exercise — vigorous enough for Pitta, not depleting for Vata
Daily meditation — calms Vata anxiety and Pitta intensity
Avoid skipping meals (Pitta) and irregular schedules (Vata)
Vata-Pitta.seasonalPriority: Watch autumn (Vata rises) and summer (Pitta rises) most carefully

Pitta-Kapha.name: Pitta-Kapha
Pitta-Kapha.heroDescription: You are built like a champion — you have the fire to drive and the earth to endure. You get things done and you build things that last. Physically you tend to be strong and capable. Your challenges are that Pitta can make you intense and critical, while Kapha can make you resistant to change. Together, you are a force of nature when balanced.
Pitta-Kapha.primaryBalance: Cool the Pitta, lighten the Kapha. Cooling foods + vigorous exercise is your daily medicine.
keyPractices: Vigorous daily exercise (Kapha) without overheating (Pitta)
Light, cooling foods that also stimulate metabolism
Avoid heavy, oily foods — doubles down on both doshas
Compassion practices to soften Pitta's edge
Regular change and new experiences to counter Kapha inertia
Pitta-Kapha.seasonalPriority: Watch summer (Pitta rises) and spring (Kapha rises) most carefully

Vata-Kapha.name: Vata-Kapha
Vata-Kapha.heroDescription: You are a fascinating paradox — the restless wind living in a steady, grounded body. Your mind loves variety and change, but your body craves stability and comfort. You are deeply creative and deeply caring. Your challenge is that these two doshas often pull in opposite directions — learning to harmonize them is your unique journey.
Vata-Kapha.primaryBalance: Warm and regulate. Regular, warm, light meals are the cornerstone of Vata-Kapha balance.
keyPractices: Warm, light, and regular meals (balances both doshas)
Gentle but consistent exercise — not too intense, not too light
Regular schedule grounds Vata without burdening Kapha
Warming spices in food help both doshas
Social connection is important — Kapha needs community, Vata needs stimulation
Vata-Kapha.seasonalPriority: Watch autumn/winter (both can rise together) most carefully

Tridoshic.name: Tridoshic
Tridoshic.heroDescription: You are extraordinarily rare — a person born with all three doshas in near-perfect balance. This is considered the most robust and resilient constitution in Ayurveda. You have the adaptability of Vata, the drive of Pitta, and the endurance of Kapha. Your challenge is that you can be affected by any dosha imbalance — especially with seasonal changes.
Tridoshic.primaryBalance: Eat and live seasonally. Your greatest tool is adjusting your diet and routine with each season.
keyPractices: Follow seasonal guidelines closely — your biggest lever
Eat a varied, balanced diet with all six tastes
Moderate, varied exercise — your body handles most types
Stay attuned to which dosha is rising and adjust accordingly
Annual Panchakarma cleanse is particularly beneficial
Tridoshic.seasonalPriority: Follow dosha-specific seasonal guidance for whichever dosha is currently dominant

sixTastes.description: Ayurveda identifies six tastes (Shad Rasa) that each affect the doshas differently

dinacharya.description: Daily routine (Dinacharya) is one of the most powerful Ayurvedic tools for all types

universalPractices.benefit: Uses Kapha time for productive movement
